task_completion.description: Navigate to the OpenCAD system login page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the OpenCAD login screen with the URL http://localhost visible in the address bar.

task_completion.description: Log into the system using the provided administrator credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenCAD dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating a successful login (the email 'admin@opencad.local' may be visible in the profile/settings area or the login form is shown filled out just prior to submission).

task_completion.description: Access the Data Manager or Weapons section of the OpenCAD system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page or tab titled 'Data Manager', 'Weapons', or similar is active, showing a list of weapons or a weapon management interface.

task_completion.description: Enter the new weapon's name and type into the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Add Weapon' form or modal is open with 'Remington 870 Breacher' entered in the Name field and 'Shotgun' entered or selected in the Type field.

task_completion.description: Submit the form to add the weapon to the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification (e.g., 'Weapon added successfully') is displayed, or 'Remington 870 Breacher' with type 'Shotgun' is newly visible as an entry in the weapons list/table.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenCAD web interface via a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser (e.g., Chromium, Firefox) being used to navigate the OpenCAD UI, rather than API testing tools like Postman.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing `curl` commands, direct database SQL inserts, or automated scripts used to add the weapon.

integrity.description: The agent entered the data into the actual application forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'Remington 870 Breacher' and 'Shotgun' are typed directly into the OpenCAD web forms, not just written in a separate text editor or notepad.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected weapon name is 'Remington 870 Breacher' and the expected type is 'Shotgun'. Verify that these exact strings are entered into the OpenCAD Data Manager interface. The system is a synthetic OpenCAD environment.
